questions.question: Select the option that best describes the SYNONYMS of the word “Pertinent.”
questions.explanation: C) Pertinent (adjective) – relevant, applicable, apposite, germane, appropriate. (प्रासंगिक)

questions.question: Select the option that best describes the meaning of the phrase “Paint a picture.”
questions.explanation: C) Paint a picture (phrase) – describe vividly, depict, portray, illustrate. (चित्रण करना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Deride.”
questions.explanation: C) Deride (verb) - mock, ridicule, sneer, taunt, disparage. (उपहास उड़ाना) Antonym: Revere (verb) – To feel deep respect or admiration for something or someone. (आदर करना) • Indict (verb): Formally accuse of or charge with a serious crime or legal violation. (दोषी ठहराना) • Garble (verb): Reproduce a message or sound in a confused and distorted way. (विकृत करना) • Quash (verb): Reject or void by legal procedure; put an end to forcefully. (कुचल देना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Coerce.”
questions.explanation: D) Coerce (verb) – force, compel, pressure, bulldoze, obligate. (मजबूर करना या जबरदस्ती करना) Synonym: Compel (verb) – Force or oblige someone to do something. (विवश करना) Tepid (adjective): Showing little enthusiasm; only slightly warm. (उत्साहहीन / गुनगुना) Recoil (verb): Suddenly spring or flinch back in fear, horror, or disgust. (पीछे हटना) Detail (verb): To describe item by item; give the full particulars of. (विस्तार से बताना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Sequestration.”
questions.explanation: D) Sequestration (noun) – isolation, segregation, separation, capturing, detaining. (पृथक्करण या भंडारण) Antonym: Unification (noun) – The process of being united or made into a whole. (एकीकरण) Aberration (noun): A departure from what is normal, usual, or expected. (विपथन / विचलन) Retribution (noun): Punishment inflicted on someone as vengeance for a wrong or criminal act. (प्रतिफल / दण्ड) Oscillation (noun): Movement back and forth at a regular speed. (हिचकिचाहट / दोलन)

questions.question: Select the option that best describes the meaning of the phrase “Of late.”
options: to refuse to accept a request or an offer
to reach a point where no progress is made
to support someone who is being criticized
recently or in the recent period of time
questions.explanation: D) Of late (phrase) – recently or in the recent period of time. (हाल ही में)

questions.question: Choose the correct one-word substitute for: A feeling of apprehension in tense situations.
questions.explanation: C) Trepidation (noun) – A feeling of apprehension or fear in tense situations घबराहट/आशंका • Elation (noun) – great happiness, उत्साह/प्रसन्नता • Fury (noun) – wild or violent anger, प्रचंड क्रोध • Serenity (noun) – calmness, शांति/स्थिरता

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word
questions.explanation: D) The correct spelling of the word in question is 'Minuscule the correct option is D. Minuscule. एक प्रकार का हस्तलेख

questions.question: Choose the correct one-word substitute for: A person who is a lover of fine arts
questions.explanation: A) Aesthete (noun) – A person who loves and appreciates fine arts सुंदर कला-प्रेमी • Artist (noun) – one who produces art कला-निर्माता • Sculptor (noun) – one who makes sculptures मूर्तिकार • Painter (noun) – one who paints चित्रकार

questions.question: Convert the sentence provided below from its passive voice structure to an active voice structure: The invitation will have been sent by the secretary before noon.
options: The secretary sends the invitation before noon.
The secretary has sent the invitation before noon.
The secretary will have sent the invitation before noon.
The secretary will be sending the invitation before noon.
questions.explanation: C) ‘The invitation will have been sent by the secretary before noon.’ का Active Voice होगा ‘The secretary will have sent the invitation before noon.’ क्योंकि Future Perfect Tense के Active Voice में Subject + will have + V3 + Object का प्रयोग होता है। • Rule: Future Perfect Passive: Object + will have been + V3 + by + Subject → Active: Subject + will have + V3 + Object. A is incorrect – ‘sends’ is Simple Present tense, which does not match the Future Perfect tense of the original sentence. B is incorrect – ‘has sent’ is Present Perfect tense, not Future Perfect. D is incorrect – ‘will be sending’ is Future Continuous tense, not Future Perfect.

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: B) The correctly spelt word is “Abeyance”, which means a state of temporary suspension or inactivity अस्थायी स्थगन / लंबित स्थिति।

questions.question: Convert the sentence provided below from its passive voice structure to an active voice structure: The rare manuscripts had been preserved meticulously by the archivists before the fire broke out.
options: The archivists have preserved the rare manuscripts meticulously before the fire broke out.
The archivists preserved the rare manuscripts meticulously before the fire broke out.
The archivists had preserved the rare manuscripts meticulously before the fire broke out.
The archivists has preserved the rare manuscripts meticulously before the fire broke out.
questions.explanation: C) ‘The rare manuscripts had been preserved meticulously by the archivists before the fire broke out.’ का Active Voice होगा ‘The archivists had preserved the rare manuscripts meticulously before the fire broke out.’ क्योंकि Past Perfect Tense के Active Voice में Subject + had + V3 + Object का प्रयोग होता है। • Rule: Past Perfect Passive: Object + had been + V3 + by + Subject → Active: Subject + had + V3 + Object. A is incorrect – ‘have preserved’ is Present Perfect tense, not Past Perfect. B is incorrect – ‘preserved’ is Simple Past tense, not Past Perfect. D is incorrect – ‘has preserved’ is grammatically wrong as the subject ‘the archivists’ is plural; moreover, it is Present Perfect, not Past Perfect.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate word. The heavy cargo vehicles faced extreme structural strain while navigating the __________ terrain of the valley, which was filled with continuous ridges and deep troughs.
questions.explanation: A) Undulating (adjective) - rolling, waving, rippling, billowing, sinuous. (उतार-चढ़ावदार) The context outlines a physical landscape defined by alternating highs and lows ("ridges and deep troughs"), which exactly matches the definition of "undulating." Omniscient (adjective): Knowing everything; possessing infinite awareness. (सर्वज्ञानी) Pretentious (adjective): Attempting to impress by affecting greater importance than is possessed. (दिखावटी) Magnanimous (adjective): Very generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival. (उदार)

questions.question: Select the correct alternative to fill in the blank with the correct preposition in the following sentence. The young man resorted to frequent outings in pursuit ______ happiness.
questions.explanation: B) "In pursuit of" सही idiomatic expression है। "The young man resorted to frequent outings in pursuit of happiness." — "Pursuit" Noun के बाद हमेशा "of" का प्रयोग होता है। for, at, और in — ये सभी इस संदर्भ में अनुपयुक्त हैं। "In pursuit of" is the correct idiomatic expression. The noun "pursuit" is always followed by the preposition "of" to indicate what is being sought or chased. Options for, at, and in are all incorrect in this context. ✅ Correct answer: of

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of homonyms can be used to fill in the blanks most suitably? She _____ the obligation of attending the kitty party with patience, but soon the mindless chatter began to _____ her.
questions.explanation: D) ‘bore , bore’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि पूरे संदर्भ में sentence एक ही क्रिया (to bore) के दो अलग अर्थों को दिखा रहा है — एक मानसिक सहनशीलता के संदर्भ में और दूसरा ऊब पैदा करने के संदर्भ में। यहाँ "She bore the obligation…" में bore का अर्थ है सहन करना / निभाना, जबकि “…began to bore her” में bore का अर्थ है उबाऊ लगना / बोर करना। इसलिए ‘bore , bore’ सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प होगा। • ‘bore , bore’ should be used because in the entire context, the sentence uses the same verb (to bore) in two different senses — first in the sense of enduring/putting up with, and second in the sense of causing boredom. Here, “She bore the obligation…” conveys endurance, while “…began to bore her” conveys causing boredom. Thus, ‘bore , bore’ would be the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The hunters waited patiently for the duck to land.
options: The mother duck led her ducklings to the water.
The children loved to feed the ducks at the park.
He had to duck to avoid the low-hanging branch.
The artist painted a beautiful picture of a duck.
questions.explanation: C) Highlighted वाक्य में ‘duck’ का अर्थ है — एक पक्षी। विकल्प c में ‘duck’ का अर्थ है — अचानक झुकना या सिर/शरीर को नीचे करना ताकि किसी चीज़ से बचा जा सके। उच्चारण और वर्तनी समान हैं, लेकिन अर्थ भिन्न हैं — इसलिए यह homonym का सही उदाहरण है। In the highlighted sentence, ‘duck’ refers to a bird. In option C, ‘duck’ means to lower oneself quickly to avoid something. Same spelling and pronunciation but different meanings — therefore option C is correct.

questions.question: Given below are six sentences taken from a paragraph. The first and the last sentences are numbered S1 and S2 and are placed in the first and last positions respectively and are fixed in their positions. The other four sentences between the first and last sentences are jumbled up and are numbered A, B, C, and D. Rearrange the jumbled sentences in a logical sequence keeping the unity and coherence of the paragraph intact. Choose the correct alternative that best describes the sequence. S1. Odette continued to look out of the window. A. It fell near the feet of the singer who, raising his eyes and seeing the fair hair of the girl, kissed the sprig and turned away, though he stopped at every step to look back. B. She gave thanks to heaven, she knew not why; she felt happy, though she did not suspect the reason of her happiness. C. She let slip the flower from her hand, still wet with her tears. D. After he had disappeared, Odette went to her prie-dieu and prayed a long time. S2. And that night she dreamed a beautiful dream.
questions.explanation: A) सही क्रम CADB है। S1 में Odette खिड़की से बाहर देख रही है। C में वह अपने हाथ से फूल गिरा देती है। A में फूल गायक के पैरों के पास गिरता है जो उसे चूमकर चला जाता है। D में उसके जाने के बाद Odette प्रार्थना करती है। B में वह खुश महसूस करती है। S2 में वह सुंदर सपना देखती है। A) The correct sequence is CADB: S1 sets the scene (Odette looking out). C follows—she lets the flower slip from her hand. A shows the flower falling near the singer who kisses it and leaves. D continues—after he disappeared, Odette prays. B concludes—she feels happy without knowing why, leading into S2 where she dreams a beautiful dream.

questions.question: In the given question, a sentence is divided into four parts and given in the alternatives (A), (B), (C), and (D). There is an error in one part. Choose the alternative containing that part of the sentence that has the error. The Golden Temple was crowded by millions of devotees from all around the world on the day of Baisakhi.
questions.explanation: B) वाक्य में त्रुटि "crowded by millions of devotees" में है। सही प्रयोग "crowded with millions of devotees" होना चाहिए। "Crowded" विशेषण के साथ "with" का प्रयोग होता है, न कि "by" का। जैसे — The room was crowded with people। The error is in "crowded by millions of devotees." The correct preposition is "with," not "by." The adjective "crowded" is always followed by "with" to indicate what fills the space. The correct sentence is: "The Golden Temple was crowded with millions of devotees..."

questions.question: A part of the following sentence is underlined. Choose the option which can replace the underlined part to improve the sentence. In case Options A, B and C are not found suitable, choose Option D: "No improvement Required." The economic reforms introduced last year are aimed at bringing up the overall productivity of the nation.
questions.explanation: B) "Bringing up" के स्थान पर "bringing about" सही है। "Aimed at bringing about" सही expression है क्योंकि "bring about" का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को उत्पन्न करना या घटित कराना। "Bring up" का अर्थ है पालना-पोसना या किसी विषय को उठाना — जो यहाँ अनुपयुक्त है। "aimed in" और "aimed for" — दोनों गलत prepositions हैं; "aimed at" ही सही है। "Bringing about" should replace "bringing up." "Bring about" means to cause or produce something (e.g., increased productivity). "Bring up" means to raise a child or mention a topic — both meanings are wrong here. Also, "aimed in" and "aimed for" use incorrect prepositions; "aimed at" is the correct form. ✅ Correct answer: aimed at bringing about

questions.question: A part of the following sentence is underlined. Choose the option which can replace the underlined part to improve the sentence. In case Options A, B and C are not found suitable, choose Option D: "No Improvement Required." The members of the club have been waiting eagerly for the chief guest to arrive since two hours.
options: to the chief guest's arrival since two hours
for the chief guest to arrive for two hours
for the chief guest's arrival from two hours
questions.explanation: B) "Since two hours" के स्थान पर "for two hours" सही है। नियम: "Since" का प्रयोग किसी निश्चित समय बिंदु के साथ होता है (since 9 AM, since Monday), जबकि "for" का प्रयोग समयावधि के साथ होता है (for two hours, for three days)। "Two hours" एक अवधि है, इसलिए "for" सही preposition है। "For two hours" should replace "since two hours." Rule: "Since" is used with a specific point in time (since 9 AM, since Monday), while "for" is used with a duration (for two hours, for three days). As "two hours" is a duration, "for" is the correct preposition. ✅ Correct answer: for the chief guest to arrive for two hours

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The Jaipur Literature Festival, often described as the “greatest literary show on Earth,” is an annual celebration of literature, ideas, and culture. It began in 2006 with modest gatherings but has now grown into a global platform where writers, thinkers, historians, politicians, business leaders, and entertainers come together. Thousands of visitors from India and abroad attend the festival every year, eager to listen to renowned authors and to engage in debates on contemporary issues. The festival has provided a stage for Nobel laureates, Booker Prize winners, and debut novelists alike. It has encouraged dialogue on topics as varied as climate change, gender equality, history, art, and technology. Over the years, JLF has’@xpanded to cities across the world, yet Jaipur remains its most iconic venue. The setting of the Diggi Palace, with itsvibrant decorations, adds charm and grandeur to the intellectual exchange. In the past, literary festivals were considered exclusive events for scholars, but JLF haSdemocratized literature by making it accessible to all. In the present day, it represents a space where people from diverse backgrounds can share stories and ideas. In the future, the festival may grow even further, incorporating digital platforms to reach audiences who cannot be physically present. Thus, JLF is not merely a festival; it is a cultural phenomenon that continues to inspire readers and writers across generations. Change the voice of the sentence. “Thousands of visitors from India and abroad attend the festival every year.”
options: The festival was attended by thousands of visitors from India and abroad every year.
Thousands of visitors are attending the festival every year from India and abroad.
Every year the festival is attended by thousands of visitors from India and abroad.
The festival had been attended by thousands of visitors from India and abroad every year.
questions.explanation: C) ‘Every year the festival is attended by thousands of visitors from India and abroad’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि दिए गए वाक्य “Thousands of visitors from India and abroad attend the festival every year” simple present tense (active voice) में है। Passive voice में बदलते समय object “the festival” subject बन जाता है और verb “attend” का passive रूप “is attended” होता है। साथ ही “every year” को शुरुआत में रखना sentence को अधिक natural बनाता है। इसलिए option 3 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘Every year the festival is attended by thousands of visitors from India and abroad’ should be used because the given sentence is in simple present tense (active voice). While converting into passive voice, the object “the festival” becomes the subject, and the verb “attend” changes into “is attended.” Also, placing “every year” at the beginning makes the sentence more natural and correct. Thus, option 3 is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The Jaipur Literature Festival, often described as the “greatest literary show on Earth,” is an annual celebration of literature, ideas, and culture. It began in 2006 with modest gatherings but has now grown into a global platform where writers, thinkers, historians, politicians, business leaders, and entertainers come together. Thousands of visitors from India and abroad attend the festival every year, eager to listen to renowned authors and to engage in debates on contemporary issues. The festival has provided a stage for Nobel laureates, Booker Prize winners, and debut novelists alike. It has encouraged dialogue on topics as varied as climate change, gender equality, history, art, and technology. Over the years, JLF has expanded to cities across the world, yet Jaipur remains its most iconic venue. The setting of the Diggi Palace, with its vibrant decorations, adds charm and grandeur to the intellectual exchange. In the past, literary festivals were considered exclusive events for scholars, but JLF has democratized literature by making it accessible to all. In the present day, it represents a space where people from diverse backgrounds can share stories and ideas. In the future, the festival may grow even further, incorporating digital platforms to reach audiences who cannot be physically present. Thus, JLF is not merely a festival; it is a cultural phenomenon that continues to inspire readers and writers across generations. The antonym of exclusive is ________.
questions.explanation: D) ‘Inclusive’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि “exclusive” का अर्थ होता है सीमित या केवल कुछ लोगों तक restricted, जबकि “inclusive” का अर्थ होता है सभी को शामिल करने वाला। इसलिए दोनों शब्द एक-दूसरे के विपरीत (antonym) हैं। इसलिए option 4 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘Inclusive’ should be used because “exclusive” means restricted or limited to a select group of people, whereas “inclusive” means involving or including everyone. Thus, both words are opposite in meaning, making option 4 the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The Jaipur Literature Festival, often described as the “greatest literary show on Earth,” is an annual celebration of literature, ideas, and culture. It began in 2006 with modest gatherings but has now grown into a global platform where writers, thinkers, historians, politicians, business leaders, and entertainers come together. Thousands of visitors from India and abroad attend the festival every year, eager to listen to renowned authors and to engage in debates on contemporary issues. The festival has provided a stage for Nobel laureates, Booker Prize winners, and debut novelists alike. It has encouraged dialogue on topics as varied as climate change, gender equality, history, art, and technology. Over the years, JLF has expanded to cities across the world, yet Jaipur remains its most iconic venue. The setting of the Diggi Palace, with its vibrant decorations, adds charm and grandeur to the intellectual exchange. In the past, literary festivals were considered exclusive events for scholars, but JLF has democratized literature by making it accessible to all. In the present day, it represents a space where people from diverse backgrounds can share stories and ideas. In the future, the festival may grow even further, incorporating digital platforms to reach audiences who cannot be physically present. Thus, JLF is not merely a festival; it is a cultural phenomenon that continues to inspire readers and writers across generations. Identify the tense in the sentence. “It began in 2006 with modest gatherings but has now grown into a global platform.”
questions.explanation: A) Simple Past + Present Perfect’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि वाक्य में दो अलग-अलग tenses का उपयोग हुआ है। “It began in 2006” में Simple Past tense (began) प्रयोग हुआ है, जो किसी निश्चित समय में शुरू हुई घटना को दर्शाता है। वहीं “has now grown” में Present Perfect tense (has grown) प्रयोग हुआ है, जो बताता है कि वह घटना अतीत में शुरू होकर वर्तमान तक जारी है। इसलिए option 1 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘Simple Past + Present Perfect’ should be used because the sentence contains two different tenses. “It began in 2006” uses the Simple Past tense, indicating an action that started at a definite time in the past, while “has now grown” uses the Present Perfect tense, showing that the action began in the past and continues into the present. Thus, option 1 is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The Jaipur Literature Festival, often described as the “greatest literary show on Earth,” is an annual celebration of literature, ideas, and culture. It began in 2006 with modest gatherings but has now grown into a global platform where writers, thinkers, historians, politicians, business leaders, and entertainers come together. Thousands of visitors from India and abroad attend the festival every year, eager to listen to renowned authors and to engage in debates on contemporary issues. The festival has provided a stage for Nobel laureates, Booker Prize winners, and debut novelists alike. It has encouraged dialogue on topics as varied as climate change, gender equality, history, art, and technology. Over the years, JLF has expanded to cities across the world, yet Jaipur remains its most iconic venue. The setting of the Diggi Palace, with its vibrant decorations, adds charm and grandeur to the intellectual exchange. In the past, literary festivals were considered exclusive events for scholars, but JLF has democratized literature by making it accessible to all. In the present day, it represents a space where people from diverse backgrounds can share stories and ideas. In the future, the festival may grow even further, incorporating digital platforms to reach audiences who cannot be physically present. Thus, JLF is not merely a festival; it is a cultural phenomenon that continues to inspire readers and writers across generations. Choose the synonym of ‘democratized.’
questions.explanation: B) Made accessible’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि “democratized” का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज़ को सभी लोगों के लिए उपलब्ध या सुलभ बनाना। Passage में भी JLF के संदर्भ में बताया गया है कि उसने साहित्य को आम लोगों तक पहुंचाया। इसलिए “made accessible” इसका सही समानार्थी (synonym) है। इसलिए option 2 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘Made accessible’ should be used because “democratized” means making something available or accessible to everyone. In the passage, it refers to how JLF has made literature open and reachable to the general public. Thus, option 2 is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The Jaipur Literature Festival, often described as the “greatest literary show on Earth,” is an annual celebration of literature, ideas, and culture. It began in 2006 with modest gatherings but has now grown into a global platform where writers, thinkers, historians, politicians, business leaders, and entertainers come together. Thousands of visitors from India and abroad attend the festival every year, eager to listen to renowned authors and to engage in debates on contemporary issues. The festival has provided a stage for Nobel laureates, Booker Prize winners, and debut novelists alike. It has encouraged dialogue on topics as varied as climate change, gender equality, history, art, and technology. Over the years, JLF has expanded to cities across the world, yet Jaipur remains its most iconic venue. The setting of the Diggi Palace, with its vibrant decorations, adds charm and grandeur to the intellectual exchange. In the past, literary festivals were considered exclusive events for scholars, but JLF has democratized literature by making it accessible to all. In the present day, it represents a space where people from diverse backgrounds can share stories and ideas. In the future, the festival may grow even further, incorporating digital platforms to reach audiences who cannot be physically present. Thus, JLF is not merely a festival; it is a cultural phenomenon that continues to inspire readers and writers across generations. According to the passage, why is Jaipur considered the most iconic venue for JLF?
options: Because it has the largest halls
Because Diggi Palace adds charm and grandeur to the exchange of ideas
Because most Nobel laureates live there
Because other cities do not host it anymore
questions.explanation: B) ‘Because Diggi Palace adds charm and grandeur to the exchange of ideas’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि Jaipur का Diggi Palace अपनी vibrant सजावट और भव्यता के कारण इस festival को विशेष बनाता है। यही कारण है कि Jaipur को JLF का सबसे iconic venue माना जाता है। इसलिए option 2 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘Because Diggi Palace adds charm and grandeur to the exchange of ideas’ should be used because the passage clearly mentions that the setting of Diggi Palace, with its vibrant decorations, enhances the charm and grandeur of the intellectual exchange. This is why Jaipur is considered the most iconic venue for JLF. Thus, option 2 is the most appropriate choice.
